title: The Wintney Version of the Rule of St Benedict
summary: The repository XML is a pinned copy of University of Michigan item AGV8488.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR encoding keyed selectively from a 206-image carrier of Arnold Schröer's 1888 bilingual edition. Schröer transcribed the alternating Latin and English Rule from the unique Cotton MS Claudius D III witness. The cataloged work is the English adaptation at current ff. 54r-140r, not the manuscript's alternating Latin text, the whole codex, the printed edition, or its digital carriers. Scholarship identifies it as a first-quarter-thirteenth-century adaptation and revision of Æthelwold's Old English Rule; that Rule in turn is a version of Benedict's Latin Rule. No exact surviving English or Latin physical exemplar is asserted. Complete BSB edition and British Library manuscript IIIF bundles, Artamonova's article, current U.S. Copyright Office term guidance, and the two reconciled GND authority records are preserved as six ignored research caches with exact checksums; access, copyright, provider conditions, and redistribution permission remain separate assertions.
review_notes: The target is one English prose adaptation comprising a prologue and chapters I-LXXIII. The XML's two chapter-seven DIV2 units, numbered 7 and 7b, are an encoding/editorial split and do not create a seventy-fourth chapter.

The manuscript target runs from the English incipit at current f. 54r through the English explicit at f. 140r. Latin and English passages alternate within that range, so canvases 117-289 are target-bearing but not English-only.
Wintney is modeled as association and provenance. The reviewed evidence does not establish that either the adaptation or the surviving copy was produced there.
Artamonova cautions that the reviser and the Cotton compiler used different Latin texts. The facing Latin in the surviving manuscript is therefore not asserted as the reviser's exact exemplar.

The BSB edition scan is a complete facsimile of the 1888 volume. Its canvas 209 reproduces only the manuscript's English incipit leaf through Schröer's drawn plate; it is a partial, second-generation witness reproduction, not a whole-manuscript facsimile.

The 1978 publication is collateral: it exactly reprints Schröer's Latin-English text and adds a new Gretsch supplement. No public exact copy was verified, and rights in the reprinted 1888 layer are not conflated with rights in the new 1978 matter.
Public access to a source or a local research cache does not itself establish permission to redistribute provider images or modern scholarship.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for agv8488
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by General Corpus and supplied through the Kyvero Press CME git submodule.
notes: The reviewed file is 220,469 bytes.
The XML contains the title page, keyed English prose, and placeholders for omitted Latin and edition matter; it is not a diplomatic transcription of the whole volume.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text AGV8488.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding created by uncorrected OCR from page images of Schröer's 1888 edition. It directly keys the title and selected English pages while representing the facing Latin and much paratext only by page-break placeholders.

date_statements.label: digital publication
date_statements.notes: The header dates publication to 2006 and says it was generated on 2006-02-22.

notes: The header's sole ger language declaration describes an edition-level catalog layer and does not override the English language of the keyed target work.

entities.label: University of Michigan 206-image carrier for AGV8488.0001.001
entities.description: The historical sequence of 206 linked 600-dpi TIFF page images from which the U-M text was OCRed and keyed. The XML preserves one PB reference per image, but current item-specific image delivery could not be fully verified because the stable item interface challenged command-line access.
entities.reproduction_format: 206 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images described in the XML header
notes: No local copy of this historical carrier was made; the independently verified BSB scan supplies reproducible edition-image evidence.

entities.label: Arnold Schröer's 1888 edition of the Winteney Rule
entities.description: The first printed edition, presenting Latin and English on alternating pages with an introduction, notes, glossary, and a drawn facsimile plate of the English manuscript incipit.

bibliographic.citation: Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti: Lateinisch und Englisch, mit Einleitung, Anmerkungen, Glossar und einem Facsimile zum erstenmale herausgegeben, ed. Arnold Schröer (Halle: Max Niemeyer, 1888).
bibliographic.title: Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti: Lateinisch und Englisch, mit Einleitung, Anmerkungen, Glossar und einem Facsimile zum erstenmale herausgegeben

notes: The English verbal text occupies printed pp. 3, 5, 7, 9 and odd pp. 13-147; the drawn facsimile plate is BSB canvas 209.
The DDB record describes the digitized exemplar as a Lumoprint copy.

entities.label: BSB complete digital facsimile of Schröer's 1888 edition
entities.description: A complete 216-canvas IIIF object for the BSB exemplar of Schröer's volume, including covers, edition matter, printed Latin and English text, the drawn manuscript plate, advertisements, and blanks.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 216 JPEG derivatives at a 1600-pixel width profile
notes: U-M TIFF reference N maps continuously to BSB canvas N+4 through U-M PB 206 / BSB canvas 210.
Canvas 209 contains Schröer's drawn reproduction of the manuscript English incipit leaf. This selected plate is evidence for only that leaf, not a complete manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Claudius D III
entities.description: A composite parchment codex containing the bilingual Rule at ff. 52r-140r, with Latin and English passages alternating. The target English adaptation begins at current f. 54r and ends at f. 140r; adjacent codex contents are distinct works.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record

date_statements.label: relevant hand
date_statements.value: early thirteenth century, probably soon after 1200
date_statements.notes: The British Library's broader c. 1220-first half of the fifteenth century range describes multiple codex layers and is not flattened into the date of this hand.

holding.notes: The codex was owned or used by Wintney Priory from the thirteenth through fifteenth centuries; this provenance does not by itself establish production there.

notes: The reviewed catalog assigns Protogothic and Gothic hands across the codex; the relevant Rule hand is separately dated by EM1060.
The manuscript's obsolete a/b foliation is one leaf behind current foliation in this section: Schröer's old 52a corresponds to current f. 54r, and old 138a to current f. 140r.

entities.label: British Library complete digital facsimile of Cotton MS Claudius D III
entities.description: The complete 349-canvas IIIF Presentation 3 object for the codex, including covers and spine. Canvases 117-289 correspond to current ff. 54r-140r and contain the target English passages interleaved with Latin.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and 349 JPEG derivatives constrained to a 1600 × 1600 pixel bounding box

entities.label: The Wintney Version of the Rule of St Benedict
alternate_labels: The Winteney Version of the Rule of St Benedict
Wintney adaptation of the Benedictine Rule
entities.description: The anonymous English prose adaptation and revision of Æthelwold's Old English Rule made for female religious readers, structured as a prologue and chapters I-LXXIII and uniquely preserved in Cotton MS Claudius D III.

date_statements.label: revision
date_statements.value: by the first quarter of the thirteenth century
date_statements.notes: The evidence permits multiple revision stages and does not identify a named adapter.

notes: Middle English is used as a discovery classification with medium confidence. The text is essentially based on an Old English translation, and scholarship warns that a rigid Old/Middle English division obscures its layered revision.

entities.label: Æthelwold's Old English translation of the Rule of St Benedict
entities.description: The Old English Benedictine Rule tradition attributed to Æthelwold that underlies the Wintney adaptation. This abstract work layer does not identify a particular surviving manuscript as the physical exemplar used by the Wintney reviser or revisers.

entities.label: Rule of St Benedict
entities.description: The Latin monastic Rule attributed to Benedict of Nursia, represented as the abstract source work behind Æthelwold's Old English translation.

entities.label: Schröer-Gretsch 1978 reprint and supplement
entities.description: A collateral publication that exactly reprints Schröer's Latin-English edition text and adds an approximately thirteen-page supplement by Mechthild Gretsch. It is not the source of the U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti, ed. Arnold Schröer, exact reprint with an Anhang by Mechthild Gretsch (Tübingen: Max Niemeyer, 1978).
bibliographic.title: Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti

notes: No public exact digital copy was found during this review.
The bibliographic identity was checked without using any name-only lifespan record to infer copyright term.

entities.label: Maria Artamonova, Construing Old English in the Thirteenth Century (2009)
entities.description: A scholarly article used to establish the target's revision date, Old/Middle English classification problem, relationship to Æthelwold's translation, possible multiple revision stages, and use of different Latin texts by reviser and compiler.

bibliographic.citation: Maria Artamonova, Construing Old English in the Thirteenth Century: The Syntax of the Winteney Adaptation of the Benedictine Rule, Leeds Studies in English, New Series 40 (2009), 27-45.
bibliographic.title: Construing Old English in the Thirteenth Century: The Syntax of the Winteney Adaptation of the Benedictine Rule

entities.label: H. Hargreaves's 1980 review of the Schröer-Gretsch edition
entities.description: A review used for the unique-witness, intended-audience, language-classification, alternating-page, and 1978 exact-reprint-plus-supplement claims.

bibliographic.citation: H. Hargreaves, review of Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti, Scriptorium 34.1 (1980), 177-178.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15T, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: Current official term guidance consulted only for the U.S. publication-based rights analysis of the 1888 edition and the published target text; it contains no copy of the target work.

entities.label: GND authority record 11708218X for Arnold Schröer
entities.description: The complete official DNB JSON-LD authority record used to reconcile Arnold Schröer's identifier and name variants with the editor named on the 1888 title page. It is a supporting authority source and contains no copy of the target work.

entities.label: GND authority record 118508911 for Benedict of Monte Cassino
entities.description: The complete official DNB JSON-LD authority record used to reconcile Benedict of Nursia with the Saint Benedict, Abbot of Monte Cassino form in the edition. It is a supporting authority source and contains no copy of the target work.

rights.component: Repository XML transcription, OCR text, annotations, and markup
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository LICENSE contains the Unlicense but repeatedly defines its subject as software. The reviewed evidence does not resolve whether inherited U-M OCR text, edition annotations, or XML markup are all within that dedication, so no blanket relicensing conclusion is drawn.
notes: The LICENSE file itself is pinned at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c, git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657, SHA-256 6b0382b16279f26ff69014300541967a356a666eb0b91b422f6862f6b7dad17e, and 1,211 bytes.
This uncertainty concerns the repository artifact layer, not the separate public-domain analysis of the medieval work or 1888 publication.

rights.component: University of Michigan digital text item
rights.basis: The current official U-M item/index layer describes the collection material as public domain and exposes the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement. The legacy XML header separately says that where applicable copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply.
notes: NoC-US is an item/provider assertion about U.S. copyright status; it is not modeled as a worldwide license or an unconditional redistribution grant.

rights.basis: The current U-M item-level public-domain/NoC-US assertion appears to cover collection materials, but the exact current image service and image-specific terms were not retrievable for direct confirmation.
notes: The conclusion is deliberately narrower than a redistribution permission and does not assume that all historical TIFF endpoints remain available.

rights.component: Medieval English verbal work as published in 1888
rights.basis: The anonymous early-thirteenth-century work was fully published in Schröer's 1888 edition. Current Copyright Office guidance says works published before 1 January 1931 are in the U.S. public domain as of 2026, subject to its stated restoration caveat; even a maximum 95-year publication term would have expired no later than 1983.
notes: This is a publication-based analysis and does not depend on identifying or calculating from any editor's lifespan.
This cataloging conclusion is not legal advice and does not determine rights in modern transcriptions, annotations, scans, or provider databases.

rights.component: Medieval English verbal work published in 1888
rights.basis: The work is anonymous and early thirteenth century, and the exact English text was fully published in 1888. The reviewed IPO guidance's special 2039 term concerns certain pre-1989 unpublished works, so it appears inapplicable to this published text layer.
notes: This cataloging conclusion is not legal advice. It is intentionally separate from British Library image rights and provider terms.

rights.basis: The source work is medieval and necessarily predates the Wintney adaptation and its 1888 publication.
The ancient Latin source work predates both medieval English versions and modern copyright terms.

rights.component: 1888 published edition text, editorial matter, typography, and plate
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1888. Current Copyright Office guidance places publications before 1 January 1931 in the U.S. public domain as of 2026, subject to the guidance's stated restoration caveat; a maximum 95-year publication term expired no later than 1983.
notes: The conclusion rests on publication date, not on an unreconciled namesake lifespan.
It does not determine provider-imposed conditions on BSB or U-M reproductions of the edition.

rights.component: Ownership and consultation of the physical manuscript object
rights.basis: Physical ownership, reading-room access, the medieval verbal work, and copyright or provider terms in digital reproductions are distinct layers.

rights.component: BSB/Google digital images and IIIF object
rights.basis: The official IIIF manifest and DDB record assign RightsStatements.org No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only. BSB's FAQ adds provider conditions for Google-digitized material and attribution expectations.
notes: NoC-NC is a rights statement, not an open license. The private local bundle's completeness does not broaden permitted reuse.
The FAQ requests attribution including the holding library, shelfmark, page or folio, URN, and digitized-by-Google wording.

rights.jurisdiction: Most countries other than the United Kingdom
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF manifest states Public Domain in most countries other than the UK. The linked manuscript-reuse help URL currently returns 404, while general British Library terms remain separately applicable to service and database use.
notes: The item-specific territorial wording is preserved verbatim in substance rather than flattened into a worldwide public-domain claim.
No redistribution permission is inferred from public IIIF delivery or the existence of a private local cache.

rights.basis: The item-specific manifest expressly excludes the UK from its public-domain wording, and the linked image-reuse help page was unavailable. General service terms alone do not resolve image copyright status.

rights.component: Exact reprint of Schröer's 1888 edition layer
rights.basis: Hargreaves identifies this layer as an exact reprint of the 1888 edition. The retained 1888 matter has the publication-based U.S. public-domain status recorded separately for that edition.
notes: This does not extend to the new 1978 supplement, typesetting, or other new matter.

rights.component: Gretsch supplement, 1978 typesetting, and other new 1978 matter
rights.basis: The supplement and any newly created 1978 matter are distinct from the exact 1888 reprint layer. No item-specific open license or permissions statement and no public exact copy were verified.
notes: No term calculation is made from a name-only lifespan.

rights.basis: The provider PDF bears a Leeds Studies in English 2010 copyright notice and no open license was found.
notes: Free public download permits access; it is not modeled as permission to redistribute the PDF.

rights.basis: The 1980 review is modern scholarship. Public Persée HTML access was verified, but no item-specific open license or redistribution grant was established.
Circular 15T is an official work of the U.S. Copyright Office; 17 U.S.C. §105 denies U.S. copyright protection to works of the United States Government.
notes: This U.S. copyright conclusion concerns the official circular only. It neither broadens rights in other cached sources nor overrides any separate service or file-delivery conditions that may apply.

rights.basis: The complete official JSON-LD record declares a CC0 1.0 license for its authority metadata description.
notes: The CC0 assertion is limited to the GND authority-data layer and does not license the 1888 edition, target work carriers, or modern scholarship.

rights.basis: The complete official JSON-LD record declares a CC0 1.0 license for its authority metadata description.
notes: The CC0 assertion is limited to the GND authority-data layer and does not license the Latin Rule, target work carriers, or modern scholarship.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Schröer's facing Latin-even and English-odd bilingual presentation with extensive front and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: Title page and keyed English pages are transcribed; facing Latin and most paratext survive only as PB placeholders.
editorial_practices.description: The item-specific U-M coding instructions direct English-only keying while preserving the title and page topology. The XML keys the English prologue and chapters I-LXXIII and serializes omitted Latin and edition matter as page breaks.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire digital encoding, especially BODY/DIV1[@TYPE='Middle English version'] and omitted-content DIV1 units
notes: The instruction prose names printed page [1], while its TIFF list starts at page 3; the XML retains the page [1] German heading separately at TIFF 29.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed text and apparatus in Schröer's 1888 volume
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR with Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries markup and linked page-image references
editorial_practices.description: The header states that OCR errors were not corrected and that no content editing was performed. Encoded readings therefore remain representation-layer evidence and must not be treated as a newly corrected edition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: One chapter VII within the intellectual sequence I-LXXIII
editorial_practices.representation: Two consecutive DIV2 units numbered 7 and 7b
editorial_practices.description: The XML's 7/7b division is preserved as a representation-level split. Catalog structure normalizes it to one chapter VII and does not inflate the work to seventy-four chapters.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Readings, corrections, later hands, and folio transitions in Cotton Claudius D III
editorial_practices.representation: Printed English transcription with dense footnotes, old-foliation milestones, introduction, notes, and glossary
editorial_practices.description: Schröer's edition records manuscript readings and interventions in a page-level apparatus while presenting the Latin and English texts on alternating pages.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed bilingual Rule, especially English pp. 3-147 and the associated notes
notes: The relation to the witness is limited to the bilingual Rule at current ff. 52r-140r; the edition's drawn plate reproduces only the English incipit leaf.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Schröer's 1888 edition and later Gretsch scholarship
editorial_practices.representation: Exact reprint of the older text with a new approximately thirteen-page supplement
editorial_practices.description: The 1978 volume preserves the 1888 textual layer and adds distinct new scholarship. Those components remain separate for lineage and rights analysis.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole 1978 publication, with exact-reprint and new-supplement layers distinguished
notes: The 1978 volume itself was not directly inspected.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/agv8488.xml, U-M item AGV8488.0001.001.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT/TITLEPAGE; BODY/DIV1[@TYPE='Middle English version']; omitted Latin and paratext DIV1 units
evidence.summary: Pins U-M item AGV8488.0001.001 and Schröer's 1888 source, describes 206 linked 600-dpi TIFF images and uncorrected OCR, and encodes the German edition heading plus the English prologue and chapters I-LXXIII while preserving omitted Latin and edition matter mainly as page-break placeholders.
notes: Pinned CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c; git blob 57fb4998bf0d321c8e5ad12804345f1e24927483; 220,469 bytes.
The header's sole ger declaration belongs to the edition/catalog layer and is not reliable work-level language evidence for the keyed English target.

evidence.citation: XPath, attribute, page-topology, and checksum audit of the pinned agv8488.xml, performed 2026-07-15.
evidence.locator: DLPSTEXTCLASS/TEXT, especially BODY/DIV1[@TYPE='Middle English version']
evidence.summary: The target DIV1 contains 75 DIV2, 15 DIV3, 89 HEAD, 187 P, 74 PB, 231 MILESTONE, and 504 NOTE1 elements, with no L, LG, or FOREIGN. Its DIV2 sequence is one prologue, chapters 1-6, chapter 7 split as 7 and 7b, and chapters 8-73. All 206 file-wide PB REF and SEQ values are unique, and page topology separates keyed English from omitted Latin and paratext.
notes: Target English PB references are TIFFs 31, 33, 35, 37 and 41, 43, ..., 175. The edition title is separately retained at TIFF 1 and the German heading at TIFF 29.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME git repository state reviewed through the General Corpus submodule.
evidence.locator: commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c; source/CME_phase_1-2/agv8488.xml; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Establishes the source repository, pinned commit, XML git blob, and license-file identity used in this manifest.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: Complete file
evidence.summary: The repository carries the Unlicense text but expressly and repeatedly calls its subject software. Its scope over inherited OCR text, edition annotations, and XML data is therefore not silently generalized.
notes: Git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657; 1,211 bytes.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, book-by-book coding instructions for batch BY, AGV8488.
evidence.locator: En-face; Key and code the following pages; Structure; Notes; Milestones
evidence.summary: The item-specific instructions identify facing Latin-even and English-odd pages, English-only keying, the title plus odd printed pages [1]-9 and 13-147, a prologue and chapters I-LXXIII, prose treatment, and retained folio and edition-reference milestones.
notes: The prose says page [1] is in scope while the explicit TIFF list begins with 31/page 3; the XML retains page [1]'s German heading separately at TIFF 29.
The exact instructions page was retrieved and inspected during research; final parallel URL checking intermittently received HTTP 403 from the U-M host for automated requests.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Nationalbibliothek, Gemeinsame Normdatei record 11708218X for Arnold Schröer.
evidence.locator: gndIdentifier; preferredNameForThePerson; variantNameForThePerson; biographicalOrHistoricalInformation; professionOrOccupation
evidence.summary: The official authority record identifies GND 11708218X as Schröer, Arnold, includes M. M. Arnold and Arnold Michael Martin variants, and records his English-philology professorship at Freiburg and later institutions. Those features reconcile the authority record with the editor styled Dr. M. M. Arnold Schröer and Freiburg professor on the 1888 title page. The authority-description node declares CC0 1.0.
notes: The complete 7,252-byte JSON-LD export was checked for entity reconciliation and parses successfully.
No rights conclusion concerning the 1888 edition is calculated from the authority record's life dates.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Nationalbibliothek, Gemeinsame Normdatei record 118508911 for Benedict of Monte Cassino.
evidence.locator: gndIdentifier; preferredNameForThePerson; biographical authority data
evidence.summary: The official authority record identifies GND 118508911 as Benedikt, Montecassino, Abt, Heiliger, reconciling it with the Saint Benedict/Abbot of Monte Cassino form used by the edition and the abstract Latin Rule entity. The authority-description node declares CC0 1.0.
notes: The complete 24,870-byte JSON-LD export parses successfully and contains no copy of the target work.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item AGV8488.0001.001 and collection index.
evidence.locator: Item metadata, indexed English text, collection public-domain notice, and DPLA No Copyright-United States statement
evidence.summary: The official indexed item identifies Schröer's 1888 edition, places it in the Middle English corpus, exposes the keyed English text, and presents public-domain/NoC-US collection rights information.
notes: The exact stable resolver returned a Cloudflare 403 challenge to command-line inspection on 2026-07-15; the official search result and collection browse/index evidence remained available.

evidence.citation: Arnold Schröer, ed., Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti: Lateinisch und Englisch, mit Einleitung, Anmerkungen, Glossar und einem Facsimile zum erstenmale herausgegeben (Halle: Max Niemeyer, 1888), BSB bsb11663190.
evidence.locator: Title canvas 5; introduction canvases 13-32; English pp. 3, 5, 7, 9 at canvases 35, 37, 39, 41; odd pp. 13-147 at canvases 45, 47, ..., 179; plate canvas 209
evidence.summary: The complete edition establishes Schröer's title and responsibility, the bilingual alternating-page structure, one prologue and seventy-three English chapters, Cotton Claudius D III as the witness, an adaptation relationship to Æthelwold's Rule, and the drawn incipit-leaf plate.
notes: The checksum belongs to the ignored complete local IIIF research bundle; it is not a redistribution claim.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek record ZYCMC7EK7J4MAVBQZZU6HONG6YX2ZI7L for BSB bsb11663190.
evidence.locator: Title, responsibility, publication, extent, shelfmark, URN, digitization note, and rights
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1888 bibliographic entity, BSB shelfmark P.lat. 303 yd, URN urn:nbn:de:bvb:12-bsb11663190-5, Lumoprint-copy note, and No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only rights statement.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF Presentation 2 manifest for bsb11663190 and validated complete local bundle.
evidence.locator: 216 canvases; manifest license; inventory.json; images/000001.jpg-images/000216.jpg
evidence.summary: The manifest has 216 canvases and a NoC-NC rights statement. The local bundle contains the complete ordered image sequence plus manifest and inventory; ZIP CRC, set cardinality, byte counts, and per-image SHA-256 values all validated.
notes: Bundle size 103,527,101 bytes; 216 inventory rows; 218 exact unique members; IIIF image profile 1600,.

evidence.citation: Direct page-label and image comparison between agv8488.xml and the complete BSB bsb11663190 bundle, 2026-07-15.
evidence.locator: U-M PB 1-206 against BSB canvases 5-210
evidence.summary: U-M TIFF reference N maps continuously to BSB canvas N+4. This identifies the U-M carrier as the edition run from title leaf through the plate verso and maps target English pages to BSB canvases 35, 37, 39, 41; 45, 47, ..., 179; and the target-bearing plate to canvas 209.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek, Digital Collections FAQ, reuse and attribution guidance.
evidence.locator: Reuse of digitized objects, Google-digitized material, commercial use, and attribution
evidence.summary: The FAQ explains noncommercial reuse of NoC-NC material, limits commercial use where Google scans are the predominant or sole component, and requests holding-library, shelfmark, page or folio, URN, and digitized-by-Google attribution.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Claudius D III, record 040-001102527.
evidence.locator: Contents; Rule locus; languages; date; physical description; origin; provenance; access; digitised content
evidence.summary: The current catalog identifies a parchment codex from England, locates the bilingual Latin and Old English Rule at ff. 52r-140r, distinguishes adjacent contents, records Wintney ownership/provenance in the thirteenth through fifteenth centuries, and links the complete digital object.

evidence.citation: Orietta Da Rold et al., London, British Library, Cotton Claudius D. iii, The Production and Use of English Manuscripts 1060-1220.
evidence.locator: Summary; manuscript item; incipits and explicits; foliation; hand; history
evidence.summary: The scholarly record dates the relevant hand to the early thirteenth century and probably soon after 1200, gives the English incipit at current f. 54r and explicit at f. 140r, explains obsolete foliation, and distinguishes unknown origin from Wintney provenance.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Cotton MS Claudius D III and validated complete local bundle.
evidence.locator: 349 canvases; rights metadata; inventory.json; images/000001.jpg-images/000349.jpg
evidence.summary: The complete 349-canvas manifest covers the codex, covers, and spine and states Public Domain in most countries other than the UK. The local bundle's CRC, set cardinality, byte counts, and all per-image SHA-256 values validated.
notes: Bundle size 126,346,822 bytes; 349 inventory rows; 351 exact unique members; IIIF image profile !1600,1600.

evidence.citation: Folio-label, inventory, and direct image inspection of the complete British Library Cotton MS Claudius D III IIIF bundle, 2026-07-15.
evidence.locator: Canvases 113-117 and 288-290, especially canvas 117/f. 54r and canvas 289/f. 140r
evidence.summary: Canvas 113 is f. 52r, canvas 117 directly shows the English incipit at f. 54r, canvas 289 directly shows the English explicit at f. 140r, and canvas 290/f. 140v begins the following obituary calendar. English passages remain interleaved with Latin within canvases 117-289.

evidence.citation: British Library general terms of use and item-linked manuscript image reuse page status.
evidence.locator: Public-domain content, prohibited uses, database/re-circulation terms; linked manuscript-reuse page
evidence.summary: General terms say PD-marked content is believed public domain in most territories and can be used subject to the terms and disclaimers, while also restricting certain database and recirculation uses. The item-linked unpublished-manuscript reuse page returned 404 during review.
notes: The general terms are recorded as provider conditions; they are not substituted for the item manifest's territorial copyright wording.

evidence.citation: Maria Artamonova, Construing Old English in the Thirteenth Century: The Syntax of the Winteney Adaptation of the Benedictine Rule, Leeds Studies in English, New Series 40 (2009), 27-45.
evidence.locator: pp. 27-34, especially pp. 29 and 31-34; PDF copyright notice
evidence.summary: Artamonova dates the revision to the first quarter of the thirteenth century, explains the Old/Middle English classification problem, identifies the work as essentially Æthelwold's Old English translation with updated forms and rewritten chapters, allows multiple revision layers, and says the reviser and Cotton compiler used different Latin texts. The PDF bears a Leeds Studies in English 2010 copyright notice and no open license.
notes: The complete valid 24-page provider PDF is 199,408 bytes.

evidence.citation: H. Hargreaves, review of Die Winteney-Version der Regula S. Benedicti, Scriptorium 34.1 (1980), 177-178.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic header and review text
evidence.summary: Hargreaves describes an adaptation for English nuns, the unique Cotton Claudius D III witness copied soon after 1200, chapter-by-chapter Latin/English alternation, the disputed Old/Middle English classification, and the 1978 publication as an exact Schröer reprint with an approximately thirteen-page Gretsch supplement.
notes: Persée's HTML was public. Its docAsPDF endpoint returned 403 during review, so no PDF or local cache is asserted.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15T, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: Complete circular, especially pre-1978 publication-term guidance
evidence.summary: The official circular gives a maximum 95-year term for subsisting pre-1978 publication copyrights and states that works published before 1 January 1931 are in the U.S. public domain as of 2026, while noting a restoration caveat.
notes: The complete valid five-page PDF is 272,184 bytes and contains no copy of the target work.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright (term).
evidence.locator: Rules for anonymous works and certain unpublished works created before 1989, including the 2039 transitional term
evidence.summary: The official guidance explains UK duration rules and the special term that can protect certain works remaining unpublished before 1989 until 2039. Because the exact target English text was published in 1888, that unpublished-work exception appears inapplicable to the verbal work layer.
notes: The resulting medium-confidence catalog statement is not legal advice and does not govern British Library image rights.

evidence.citation: 17 U.S.C. §105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.locator: Section 105(a)
evidence.summary: Provides the statutory basis for treating official Copyright Office Circular 15T, as a federal-government work, as outside U.S. copyright protection.

evidence.citation: Exact local-copy set, checksum, format, archive-integrity, and canonical-to-worktree hard-link audit for agv8488, performed 2026-07-15.
evidence.locator: Six local_copies records and source-cache/agv8488 file set
evidence.summary: The six manifest paths exactly equal the six files in both canonical and worktree source-cache/agv8488 directories. SHA-256 and byte counts match every local-copy record; the two JSON-LD files parse; the two PDFs validate; both IIIF ZIPs have clean CRC, exact manifest/inventory/image sets, and per-image byte/SHA matches. Canonical and worktree paths are hard links on device 40 with identical inodes and link count 2.
notes: Schröer GND JSON-LD inode 24211954; Benedict GND JSON-LD inode 24211957.
Artamonova PDF inode 24208627; Copyright Office PDF inode 24208626; BSB IIIF ZIP inode 24204348; British Library IIIF ZIP inode 24207322.
Integrity and local preservation are not assertions of redistribution permission.

open_questions.question: How many historical revision stages produced the Wintney adaptation, and can any surviving Old English or Latin witness be identified as a direct physical exemplar?
next_steps: Consult Gretsch's 1978 Anglia article and Jayatilaka's full textual study.
Keep work-level agreement with Æthelwold's tradition separate from identification of a physical exemplar.
Do not identify the exact facing Latin text in Cotton Claudius D III as the reviser's exemplar; reviewed scholarship says the reviser and compiler used different Latin texts.

open_questions.question: Were the English adaptation and surviving Cotton copy produced at Wintney Priory, or does the current evidence establish only later use and ownership there?
next_steps: Review palaeographical, codicological, and institutional scholarship on the manuscript's production.
Keep Wintney as association and provenance until direct production evidence is found.

open_questions.question: Do all 206 historical U-M TIFF page images remain publicly retrievable through current item-specific endpoints?
next_steps: Retry the stable resolver and item interface without automated-client blocking.
Ask U-M Digital Collections for a current IIIF or image-service inventory if exact historical-image access becomes necessary.
Continue using the independently verified complete BSB edition facsimile for reproducible page inspection.

open_questions.question: Can an authorized or otherwise lawful exact digital copy of the 1978 reprint and Gretsch supplement be located for direct inspection?
next_steps: Search institutional catalogs and publisher successors for licensed digital access.
Inspect a library copy if the Gretsch supplement becomes necessary for a later revision-history decision.
Retain Hargreaves's review as indirect evidence and do not infer details beyond it in the meantime.
